First, the code for steps:

public class Solution {
    public int countKReducibleNumbers(String s, int k) {
        int MOD = 1_000_00_007; // Wait, 1e9+7 is 1000000007. So MOD = 1_000_000_007;

        int len = s.length();
        // Precompute steps for all m up to len (max possible set bits)
        int[] steps = new int[801]; // since len can be up to 800, m can be up to 800
        steps[1] = 0;
        for (int m = 2; m <= 800; m++) {
            int c = Integer.bitCount(m);
            steps[m] = 1 + steps[c];
        }

        // Now collect valid m's
        List<Integer> valid_m = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int m = 1; m <= len; m++) {
            if (1 + steps[m] <= k) {
                valid_m.add(m);
            }
        }

        // Now for each valid m, compute count of numbers <n with exactly m set bits
        long total = 0;
        for (int m : valid_m) {
            long cnt = countNumbersWithMSetBits(s, m);
            total = (total + cnt) % MOD;
        }

        return (int) (total % MOD);
    }

    private long countNumbersWithMSetBits(String s, int m) {
        // Implement digit DP here
        // returns the number of numbers less than s (binary) with exactly m set bits
    }
}

private long countNumbersWithMSetBits(String s, int m) {
    int len = s.length();
    Long[][][][] memo = new Long[len][m+1][2][2]; // pos, count, tight, leading_zero
    return dp(s, 0, 0, 1, 1, m, memo);
}

public class Solution {
    public int countKReducibleNumbers(String s, int k) {
        int MOD = 1_000_00_007; // Wait, 1e9+7 is 1000000007. So MOD = 1_000_000_007;

        int len = s.length();
        // Precompute steps for all m up to len (max possible set bits)
        int[] steps = new int[801]; // since len can be up to 800, m can be up to 800
        steps[1] = 0;
        for (int m = 2; m <= 800; m++) {
            int c = Integer.bitCount(m);
            steps[m] = 1 + steps[c];
        }

        // Now collect valid m's
        List<Integer> valid_m = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int m = 1; m <= len; m++) {
            if (1 + steps[m] <= k) {
                valid_m.add(m);
            }
        }

        // Now for each valid m, compute count of numbers <n with exactly m set bits
        long total = 0;
        for (int m : valid_m) {
            long cnt = countNumbersWithMSetBits(s, m);
            total = (total + cnt) % MOD;
        }

        return (int) (total % MOD);
    }

    private long countNumbersWithMSetBits(String s, int m) {
        // Implement digit DP here
        // returns the number of numbers less than s (binary) with exactly m set bits
    }
}

So for memoization, we can use a memo table with pos, count, tight (0 or 1), leading_zero (0 or 1). 

But for each m, the countNumbersWithMSetBits function will have its own memo table. 

But for each call to countNumbersWithMSetBits, we can compute the DP for that m. 

So here's the code for countNumbersWithMSetBits:

private long countNumbersWithMSetBits(String s, int m) {
    int len = s.length();
    Long[][][][] memo = new Long[len][m+1][2][2]; // pos, count, tight, leading_zero
    return dp(s, 0, 0, 1, 1, m, memo);
}
